commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=996d7b7f32881181c8f252dde14c7c0443be4ff9)
commands.review_summary:
Registers the opencode-go static model catalog during provider onboarding by exporting catalog constants and passing them to `applyProviderConfigWithModelCatalog`.

PR surface: Source +10. Total +10 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current opencode-go onboarding is a no-op, while memory flush resolves context with async loading disabled and falls back to the default when no config/cache metadata is available. I did not run a live gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because automation can repair the import, but it cannot supply the external contributor's real setup proof or decide the provider-config persistence tradeoff.

target.title: fix(opencode-go): register model catalog to fix context window detection
target.body:
## Problem

The `opencode-go` plugin's `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig` was a no-op — it just returned `cfg` unchanged. This meant the plugin's static model catalog (19+ models with correct `contextWindow` values, e.g. `deepseek-v4-pro: 1_000_000`) was never registered into the runtime model context window cache.

As a result, `resolveContextTokensForModel()` failed to find any opencode-go model and fell back to the hardcoded `200_000` default. This caused:

- `openclaw models list` showing `195k` context for 1M-window models
- Compaction threshold calculated as `200K - 24K - 20K = 156K` instead of `1M - 24K - 20K = 956K`
- Compaction firing ~6x too frequently

## Root Cause

`applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig` (in `extensions/opencode-go/onboard.ts`) was:
```ts
export function applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig(cfg: OpenClawConfig): OpenClawConfig {
  return cfg;  // ← does nothing
}
```

Compare with the deepseek plugin, which properly calls `applyProviderConfigWithModelCatalog`.

## Fix

1. **provider-catalog.ts**: Export `OPENCODE_GO_MODELS` and `OPENCODE_GO_OPENAI_BASE_URL` (previously unexported constants)
2. **onboard.ts**: Implement `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig` using `applyProviderConfigWithModelCatalog` with the full 19-model catalog

This matches the established pattern used by `deepseek` and other built-in providers.

## Verification

Before:
```bash
$ openclaw models list | grep opencode-go/deepseek
opencode-go/deepseek-v4-pro   text   195k   # wrong: should be 977k
```

After:
```bash
$ openclaw models list | grep opencode-go/deepseek  
opencode-go/deepseek-v4-pro   text   977k   # correct: 1M
```

The fix has also been tested via manual config workaround (adding `models.providers.opencode-go` with full model definitions in `openclaw.json`), confirming that the core issue is the missing provider config registration.

Closes: #92912
target.author: kumaxs
